sourceText: The greatest danger for most of us is not that our aim is too high and we miss it, but that it is too low and we reach it.

Don't let comfort be the enemy of your potential. It's better to strive for greatness and fall short than to settle for mediocrity and achieve it. Set your sights beyond your current reach; growth lies in the pursuit, not just the attainment.
If people knew how hard I worked to achieve my mastery, they wouldn't find it so wonderful at all. Genius is the result of monumental labor.

Excellence isn't accidental; it's an obsession. Giants hide their struggle behind the elegance of the result, but never forget that each masterpiece is built on relentless, often invisible, effort. Respect the process more than the praise.

Enduring the monumental poverty of his youth and the absolute rejection of the artistic establishment. Facing the terrifying challenge of losing his sight—his most vital asset—to cataracts, while attempting to finish his most monumental work of art.

Transcended the academic constraints of his era to provide a new map for human vision, proving that the eye is more durable than the object. Established a monumental standard for artistic devotion, showing that a giant’s impact is found in their ability to see the eternal in the ephemeral.
Perhaps I should have become a painter of flowers. Don't paint the thing; paint the light that falls on it. Trust your senses.

On the Sovereignty of Perception: Reality is a fleeting 'impression' of the moment. You access monumental authenticity by focusing on the immediate, sensory truth of your experience, rather than societal labels. Master your own gaze.
Color is my obsession all day, my joy and my torment. A monumental passion is the only way to achieve a monumental result. Embrace the struggle of your art.
Greatness is a 24-hour commitment. By making your obsession your primary focus, you achieve a level of mastery casual observers can't imagine. Let your passion consume you.
My only merit lies in having painted directly from nature. Simplicity is a monumental virtue; return to the source.

Influence stems from authenticity. Giants draw their monumental strength from connecting to fundamental truths. By stripping away the artificial and staying close to your field's raw reality, you preserve your work's vitality.

Enduring the monumental trauma of a mock execution and the absolute physical hardship of a Siberian prison camp. Facing the challenge of a lifelong neurological disorder and the monumental psychological burden of debt that forced him to write his masterpieces under extreme duress.

Transcended his status as a convict and a gambler to become the most profound thinker of his century, proving that the spirit can thrive in any dungeon. Established a monumental standard for psychological realism, showing that a giant’s impact is found in their ability to tell the truth about the soul.
